title.list.description: Title for the list of custom API keys.

title.addCredential.description: Button text to add a new API key.

title.editCredential.description: Button text to edit an existing API key.

title.deleteCredential.description: Button text to delete an existing API key.

description.list.message: Use your own service provider API Key (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, DeepSeek, Moonshot, Zhiyu) instead of the platform account pool. Once configured, the corresponding model's dialogue requests will directly call the upstream service provider using your Key, without consuming platform credits; tool calls, references, artifacts, cloud history, and other functions will work as usual.
description.list.description: Description explaining the use of custom API keys.

field.provider.description: Label for the service provider field.

field.labelPlaceholder.description: Placeholder text for the notes field.

field.apiKey.description: Label for the API key field.

field.apiKeyPlaceholder.description: Placeholder text for the API key input.

field.apiKeyEditPlaceholder.message: Leave empty to keep current Key unchanged
field.apiKeyEditPlaceholder.description: Placeholder text for editing the API key.

field.baseUrl.description: Label for the custom base URL field.

field.baseUrlPlaceholder.message: Leave empty to use provider's default address
field.baseUrlPlaceholder.description: Placeholder text for the custom base URL input.

field.isActive.description: Label for the active status field.

field.lastUsedAt.description: Label for the last used timestamp.

field.createdAt.description: Label for the timestamp when the API key was added.

value.active.description: Status indicating the API key is active.

value.inactive.description: Status indicating the API key is not active.

value.never.description: Status indicating the API key has never been used.

value.default.description: Status indicating the API key is the default one.

button.add.description: Button text to add a new API key.

button.edit.description: Button text to edit the selected API key.

button.test.description: Button text to test the API key connection.

button.delete.description: Button text to delete the selected API key.

button.save.description: Button text to save the API key.

button.cancel.description: Button text to cancel the action.

message.deleteConfirm.message: Confirm deletion of this API Key? Subsequent {provider} model dialogues will revert to the platform's default upstream.
message.deleteConfirm.description: Confirmation message for deleting an API key.

message.deleteSuccess.description: Message displayed after successfully deleting an API key.

message.saveSuccess.description: Message displayed after successfully saving an API key.

message.testSuccess.description: Message displayed when the API key test is successful.

message.testFailed.description: Message displayed when the API key test fails.

message.loadFailed.description: Message displayed when loading credentials fails.

message.saveFailed.description: Message displayed when saving credentials fails.

message.empty.message: No custom API Key configured yet. Click "Add API Key" to use your own service provider account.
message.empty.description: Message displayed when no custom API keys are configured.

message.advancedToggle.description: Button text to toggle advanced options.

message.baseUrlHelp.message: Optional. Custom upstream address (for OpenRouter, self-built reverse proxy, enterprise gateway, etc.). Only HTTPS is allowed, cannot point to internal/loopback addresses.
message.baseUrlHelp.description: Help text for the custom base URL field.

badge.active.description: Badge indicating that the current dialogue is using the user's own API key.

badge.tooltip.message: This dialogue uses your own {provider} API Key, costs will be settled by your {provider} account, and will not deduct from platform balance.
badge.tooltip.description: Tooltip explaining the cost settlement for using the user's own API key.
